commit.message:
ci: extract RelWithDebInfo build into build-reldebinfo prerequisite job (#290)

* ci: extract RelWithDebInfo build into build-reldebinfo prerequisite job

The three callgrind profile jobs each ran a full CMake configure+build
before profiling. The matrix job rebuilt the same bench_callgrind binary
seven times (one per fixture), even though the fixture is only a runtime
argument.

Add a new build-reldebinfo job that:
- Configures with RelWithDebInfo, BUILD_BENCHMARKS=ON, BUILD_EXAMPLES=ON,
  ENABLE_WERROR=ON, B4C_CHECK_OVERLAPS=OFF, and VALGRIND_INCLUDE_DIR.
- Builds all targets in one pass.
- Uploads bench_callgrind, bench_assembly_navigator, and exampleB4c as
  the reldebinfo-binaries artifact (retention 1 day).

All three callgrind jobs now declare needs: [build-reldebinfo], download
the artifact, and run only the profile + annotate steps inside eic_xl.

Also add -DB4C_CHECK_OVERLAPS=OFF to build-test-benchmark. This flag
only affects exampleB4c behavior and is safe to set globally.

commit.message:
bench: add NIST CTC timing benchmarks and point cloud generation (#292)

Replace the unconditional `continue` for IsImportedSelfComparisonFixture
(NIST CTC) with reduced-ray-count BM_rays and BM_inside benchmarks.
Using kNistCtcRayCount=64 rays avoids timeouts on large AP203 compound
assemblies while producing timing data and point cloud output.

Since native and imported are the same G4OCCTSolid for CTC fixtures,
mismatches are always 0.  The benchmarks wrap CompareFixtureRays in a
try-catch so a G4Exception on a pathological CTC geometry does not abort
the entire benchmark run.

commit.message:
fix: cache saved tabs at startup to prevent metadata loss during restore

When restoring tabs sequentially on startup, each OpenTab() call
triggered persistTabs() which overwrote settings.json with only
the tabs opened so far. Subsequent OpenTab() calls then couldn't
find metadata for not-yet-opened tabs — only the first tab kept
its label and color.

Fix: GetSavedTabs() now caches the original saved tab list in
savedTabCache. OpenTab() reads from this cache first (immune to
persistTabs() overwrites), falling back to current settings for
tabs opened later during normal operation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Pen uses immediate drag (no long-press)  fix pencil drag on iPad

Theory: pencil drag fails not because of compositor cancellation but
because the long-press timer never fires. Apple Pencil has natural
micro-jitter that exceeds the 6px movement threshold during the
400ms hold period, causing the gesture to become a scroll instead.

Evidence: pencil scrolling works perfectly on iPad, proving pointer
events ARE being delivered. The scroll proxy takes over because
the pen moves.

Fix: pen now uses immediate drag like mouse  movement beyond 4px
starts the drag directly. Only finger touch still uses long-press.
Ghost for pen is created on <body> with pointer capture (same
pattern as touch long-press) for iOS compositor safety.

Trade-off: pencil can no longer scroll the element panel by swiping
on thumbnails. Use finger for panel scrolling, pencil for dragging.
This matches the natural iPad UX (pencil = precision, finger = nav).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix iPad drag: overflow:hidden kills compositor scroll claim

Root cause: WebKit's compositor runs on a separate thread and
aggressively claims gestures inside overflow:auto containers for
scrolling, firing pointercancel. JS preventDefault() and CSS
touch-action on child elements cannot override this reliably,
especially for Apple Pencil (Safari treats stylus differently).

Fix: On iOS/iPadOS, set element panel (#element-list) to
overflow-y:hidden + touch-action:none. The compositor no longer
sees a scrollable container, so it never claims the gesture.
Scrolling is handled by JS: thumb-started scrolls use the existing
scroll proxy in the drag handler, non-thumb scrolls (panel
background, labels) use a new pointer-event scroll handler with
momentum.

Also removes the per-thumb touchstart handler (superseded).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Apple Pencil drag: touchstart preventDefault + drag layer pattern

Three-layer fix for WebKit compositor blocking pencil drag-to-place:
1. touchstart.preventDefault() for touchType==='stylus'  blocks
   compositor gesture handling at earliest possible point
2. Ghost created on <body> (outside scroll container) during long-press
   with touch-action:none  compositor sees non-scrollable context
3. setPointerCapture on ghost (not thumb)  pointer events route
   through drag layer, not scroll container

Also: ghost now appears immediately on long-press (cleaner UX),
pointer capture transfer from thumb to body-level ghost.

commit.message:
Unify pen/touch drag: long-press for all, scroll proxy, touchmove prevention

Pen (Apple Pencil / Surface Pen) now uses the same long-press pattern
as finger touch  hold 400ms to drag, swipe to scroll.

Fixes:
- iPad pencil: drag no longer broken by vertical movement (long-press
  avoids compositor race; touchmove.preventDefault after long-press
  prevents WebKit from taking over the gesture)
- Windows pen: can now scroll the element panel (swipe before
  long-press proxies to scrollTop, same as finger)

Three layers of scroll prevention after long-press:
1. CSS touch-action:none on thumbs
2. e.preventDefault() on pointerdown
3. touchmove.preventDefault() registered when long-press fires

Mouse behavior unchanged (immediate drag, scroll via wheel).
SW bumped to v35.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix iPad touch/pen drag: prevent compositor gesture hijack

Root cause: touch-action:pan-y on element thumbs lets the WebKit
compositor handle vertical panning, firing pointercancel and killing
drag operations for both finger and Apple Pencil.

Fix:
- CSS: touch-action:none on .element-thumb (compositor won't compete)
- JS: e.preventDefault() on pointerdown for touch/pen (belt-and-suspenders)
- JS: manual scroll passthrough for finger swipes before long-press
  (since native scrolling on thumbs is now disabled)
- JS: double-tap detection for touch/pen (replaces click synthesis
  that preventDefault blocks)
- Removed dynamic touchAction style changes (no longer needed)
- Bumped SW cache to v34

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Old formula: fill(12+tTemp*18, 14+tLight*14, 18+coolBias*12)

- Eerste verd. (18C, 422lux, 50%): fill(110, 101, 135) cooler blue-purple

fix: predator/herbivore extinction — rebalance ecosystem

Root causes:
1. Initial seeding: 12 predators = 12 herbivores per room (1:1 ratio kills herbivores fast)
2. Predator lifespan: 800-1400 frames = 13-23s (dead before they can eat)
3. Predators hunted all floors with strong force (0.35), herbivores had no escape

Fixes:
- Initial counts per room: plants=18, herbivores=8, predators=3 (was 12), decomp=6, fungus=6
- Predator lifespan: 800-1400 -> 3000-6000 frames
- Predator energy drain: -0.04/frame -> -0.015/frame
- Predator hunt radius: 110 -> 80px, steering: 0.35 -> 0.20
- Herbivore flee: stronger force (0.08 close, 0.01 far), wider range (80->100px)
- Herbivore reproduction: threshold 115->95 energy, chance 0.003->0.005
- Added checkMinPopulation(): runs every 5s, auto-spawns if species < critical minimum

commit.message:
fix: prevent concurrent settings saves from overwriting tab metadata

Root cause: multiple callers (SaveWindowState, AddRecentFile,
SaveSettings from frontend, CompleteCopilotAuth, etc.) all did
GetSettings() → modify one field → SaveSettings(), creating a
race where the Tabs slice from a stale read would overwrite
freshly-persisted tab metadata (labels, colors).

Fix: config.SaveSettings() now ALWAYS preserves current in-memory
Tabs. Only the new SaveSettingsWithTabs() (called exclusively by
persistTabs) can update the Tabs slice. This makes it impossible
for any other caller to overwrite tab data.

Also deep-copy Tabs and RecentFiles slices in GetSettings() so
callers cannot mutate shared backing arrays.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Bump the `V4` backend to IceLake/Zen4+ (#861)

This PR bumps adds the following feature requirements to
`diskann_wide::arch::x86_64::V4`:

* `avx512vpopcntdq`: 32-bit and 64-bit vectorized population counts.
* `avx512bitalg`: Even more population counts!
* `avx512vbmi`: Powerful shuffles.

With these, the `V4` backend will require IceLake/Zen4 or higher. Ice
Lake is already 7 years old - limiting ourselves to just what's
available for the `x86-64-v4` target-architecture is overly limiting.

To help with testing, this PR adds a step to CI that uses
[SDE](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/developer/articles/tool/software-development-emulator.html)
to ensure that AVX-512 code is properly tested in CI. Since we're using
SDE anyways, it also rewires the QEMU tests for baseline testing over to
SDE. The changes to `arch/x86_64/v3/masks.rs` allow the entire
`diskann-wide` test suite to pass when compiling for the `x86-64` CPU.

---------

fix: simplify tab persistence and use OS file dialog for change file

  persistTabs(). Previously it wrote frontend-supplied tabStates
  directly to settings.json, which could overwrite label/color
  with empty values (root cause of lost metadata on restart).

  the authoritative in-memory a.tabs map.

  the same directory as the current tab file. No more text input.
- Removed ChangeFileDialog.svelte component and all its wiring.
- Context menu item calls Go directly, updates store on success.

commit.message:
fix: prevent UI freeze when app is backgrounded

Root cause: requestAnimationFrame stops firing when WebKit2GTK hides
the webview (minimized, alt-tabbed away). Line events accumulated in
pendingLines with no flush, and when the window regained focus the
massive batch overwhelmed the renderer.

Fixes:
- Replace requestAnimationFrame with setTimeout(100ms) for line event
  batching — timers keep firing even when the page is hidden.
- When document is hidden, only buffer lines for the active tab and
  cap at 2000 to prevent unbounded memory growth.
- On visibility/focus regain, immediately flush any pending lines and
  force a WebKit repaint.
- Add getActiveTabId() to tab store for synchronous access.
- Guard appendLines against empty newLines arrays.
- Clean up flush timer on component destroy.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
